semantic_journey: Arabic Andalus = al-Andalus (named after the Vandals who conquered Iberia). Greek Ouandalos = Vandal (Germanic tribal name). Arabic al-Andalus was named AFTER the Vandals via Arabic borrowing of the Germanic tribal name. Proper name, Arabic is the borrower not the source.
key_insight: Arabic al-Andalus derives from the Vandal tribal name (Germanic); proper name exclusion applies.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-khushu = lowering, bodily submission, sinking down. Greek lekhessi = on beds/couches (dative plural of lekhos). Core: lowered, settled body position. Same analysis as GRC_0682. 1-step drift: bodily-lowering -> lying-down-on-beds.
key_insight: Arabic bodily-submission/lowering and Greek lying-on-beds share the lowered horizontal body position (same as GRC_0682).

semantic_journey: Arabic al-kanish = roots from which branches spread. Greek Leukanos = Lucanian (ethnic adjective from Lucania, Italy). Roots-spreading and an Italian regional ethnic name share no semantic path. Proper name exclusion applies.
key_insight: Greek Leukanos is a proper ethnic adjective from Lucania; Arabic root-spreading does not plausibly generate this toponym.

semantic_journey: Arabic saykha = firmness, embeddedness, fixed structure. Greek eskhekei = had held/possessed (pluperfect of ekhein). Core: stably settled and held. Arabic being-firmly-fixed -> Greek having-held-firmly. 1-step drift.
key_insight: Arabic firmness/embeddedness and Greek having/holding share the concept of something stably settled and possessed.

semantic_journey: Arabic daelaqa = persistent inquiry into a thing. Greek dededelokas = you have shown/made clear. Persistent inquiry and making-something-clear are cause and effect but 3+ semantic steps apart (inquiry -> finding truth -> revealing -> showing clearly). Too long.
key_insight: Arabic persistent-inquiry and Greek making-clear are cause and effect but 3+ semantic steps apart.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-samiyan = poured down on, the matter befell him. Greek leepsomene = about to take/receive/seize (future participle of lambanein). Core: something descending upon and being received. 1-step drift: matter-befalling -> being-seized-by.
key_insight: Arabic something-befalling/pouring-down and Greek about-to-receive share the concept of something descending upon a recipient.

semantic_journey: Arabic saratahu = to take and swallow (seize and consume). Greek apestereethe = was deprived of, was robbed of. Two sides of the same forcible-removal action: Arabic is the taker; Greek is the deprived. 1-step negation-cognate.
key_insight: Arabic seizing/swallowing and Greek being-deprived are two sides of the same forcible-removal action.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-shinat = well-cooked/roasted meats. Greek alaiphanto = they anointed/rubbed with oil/fat. Core: fat/oil applied to surface in preparation. Roasting involves fat; anointing involves oil. Both encode fat/oil as medium applied to a surface. 1-step shared concept: fat/oil-applied-to-surface.
key_insight: Arabic roasted-meats (fat-cooked) and Greek anointing-with-fat share fat/oil as the medium applied to a surface.
